llm_advice.rationale: Baseline ATen achieves no speedup; bottleneck is multiple kernel launches for W@X, B.T@X, A@(B.T@X), and add. Fusing the rank-16 path (B.T@X, A@result, and addition) into a single custom kernel while leaving the large W@X to cuBLAS reduces launch overhead and improves memory locality. Scalar, vectorized, and block-size variants will be explored sequentially.

notes: Use ATen/cuBLAS for the d x d GEMM.
Explore kernels that fuse Y += A @ T for rank 16.
